title: Diagnose a slow query with explain executionStats
description: Read totalDocsExamined versus nReturned to tell whether a query is index-bound or scanning the collection.
body: Use when a find or aggregation is unexpectedly slow. Actions: run the query with .explain("executionStats"), read the winning plan's stage (IXSCAN good, COLLSCAN bad), and compare totalDocsExamined and totalKeysExamined to nReturned; a large ratio means the index is missing or unselective. Add or reorder a compound index to close the gap and re-run explain. Verify the examined-to-returned ratio falls toward one. Validation signals include the stage flipping from COLLSCAN to IXSCAN and totalDocsExamined approaching nReturned. Risk boundary: adding an index fixes reads but taxes writes and RAM, so confirm with $indexStats that existing indexes are used before piling on another.
